initial_request_score.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: BRAF V600E is a constitutive BRAF mutation that accounts for more than 90% of BRAF mutations in melanoma.
In this review, we summarize the mechanism and the consequences of BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib resistance in melanoma, aiming to provide a guidance for the intervention of BRAF inhibitor resistance.

issues.claim: Provide guidance to address the 50% resistance rate and 6-month disease progression
issues.reason: The text states the 50% rate and 6-month progression as existing problems, not as a quantifiable goal of the review itself to 'address'. The goal is to 'provide guidance'.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Change quantifiable_goal to 'Summarize mechanisms to guide intervention for the reported 50% resistance rate and 6-month progression'.

step_scores.label: partially_supported

evidence.quote: vemurafenib treatment-induced resistance occurs in about 50% of patients diagnosed with melanoma, and half of the patients have disease progression within six months.

issues.reason: The paper is a Review Article ('In this review, we summarize...'), it did not perform a clinical trial itself.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Change action to 'literature_review' and tool to 'Data Extraction'.

evidence.quote: reactivation of oncogenic signaling using the MAPK pathway in approximately two-thirds of cases

issues.reason: The paper summarizes existing literature on pathways, it did not perform a new pathway analysis tool run.

evidence.quote: most BRAF V600E mutant melanomas not responding to vemurafenib are also cross-resistant to single-agent MEK inhibitors

issues.claim: action: wet_experiment, tool: Cell Line Testing
issues.reason: The paper cites Atefi et al. (2011) for this data, it did not perform the cell line testing itself.

evidence.quote: EGFR–SFK–STAT3 signaling pathway was upregulated in cells with acquired resistance to vemurafenib

issues.claim: action: wet_experiment, tool: Resistant Cell Line Generation
issues.reason: The paper cites Girotti et al. (2013) and Becker et al. (2014), it did not generate cell lines itself.

evidence.quote: STAT3-targeted therapies are effective in cells that have acquired resistance to the BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib

issues.reason: The paper reviews findings from Lesinski (2013) and Liu et al. (2013), it did not administer inhibitors itself.

evidence.quote: Treatment with a STAT3 inhibitor and silencing STAT3 via siRNA was effective in inhibiting growth

issues.reason: The paper cites Liu et al. (2013), it did not perform siRNA silencing itself.

evidence.quote: The inhibition of the TNFα/NF-κB pathway and CD271 silencing restore the BRAFi sensitivity

issues.reason: The paper cites Lehraiki et al. (2015), it did not perform pathway inhibition itself.

evidence.quote: CD271 silencing restore the BRAFi sensitivity of resistant melanoma cells

issues.reason: The paper cites Lehraiki et al. (2015), it did not perform silencing itself.

evidence.quote: PYK2 kinase was activated by vemurafenib treatment and was required for vemurafenib-induced invadopodia formation

issues.claim: action: wet_experiment, tool: Kinase Activation Analysis
issues.reason: The paper cites Shen et al. (2021) and Yan et al. (2023), it did not perform kinase analysis itself.

evidence.quote: Identification of potential targets for vemurafenib combination therapy is urgent and will improve the therapeutic efficiency

issues.claim: action: dry_experiment, tool: Combination Therapy Design
issues.reason: The paper proposes this in the conclusion as future work, it did not perform a design experiment itself.

evidence.quote: In this review, we summarize the mechanism and the consequences of BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib resistance
resistance occurs in about 50% of patients... progression within six months... MAPK pathway in approximately two-thirds of cases
Identification of potential targets for vemurafenib combination therapy is urgent... Further investigations... are needed
